docs.title: About
docs.text:
See installation tab for setting things up

Nami is a real-time 2D wave simulation module for Roblox built on EditableMesh. It simulates wave propagation using the discrete wave equation and renders the result as a deformable water surface.

docs.title: The Math
docs.text:
The 2D wave equation is given by:

\[ \frac{\partial^2 u}{\partial t^2} = c^2 \left( \frac{\partial^2 u}{\partial x^2} + \frac{\partial^2 u}{\partial z^2} \right) \]

Where u(x,z,t) is a function of water height at point (x,z) during time t.

The terms to the right resemble the curvature of the surface and are approximated with central finite differences. The second time derivative is then solved using Verlet integration:

\[ u_{t+\Delta t} = 2u_t - u_{t-\Delta t} + a_t \Delta t^2 \]

docs.title: Complete Example
docs.text:
local Nami = require(script.NamiFinal)
local ocean: Nami.WaterSurface = Nami.create(50,50, 1, 30, 1/50, 0.98,1)
ocean:initialise(workspace)
ocean.meshPart.Material = Enum.Material.Glass

game.RunService.RenderStepped:Connect(function(dt)
    local t = os.clock()
    if ocean:step(dt) then
        local z,x = ocean:worldPositionToMesh(math.sin((2*math.pi)/2 * t)*10, math.cos((2*math.pi)/2 * t)*10)
        ocean:pointSplash(z,x,5)
    end
end)

docs.title: WaterSurface API
docs.text: Method initialise(parent) step(deltaTime) pointSplash(z, x, height) smoothSplash() normalAt(z, x) heightAt(z, x) worldPositionToMesh(z, x) destroy()

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
Place the Nami ModuleScript somewhere accessible and require it: 

local Nami = require(path.To.Nami)
It is recommended that Nami is ran on the client.

docs.title: Nami.create()
docs.text: Creates a new water surface instance.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text:
local surface = Nami.create(
    lengthX,
    lengthZ,
    vertsPerStud,
    waveSpeed,
    timeStep,
    damping,
    blurSigma
)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Units Description lengthX number Studs Width of the water surface. lengthZ number Studs Length of the water surface. vertsPerStud number Vertex per Stud Vertex density used for rendering. waveSpeed number Studs per Second Speed at which waves propagate. timeStep number Seconds Fixed simulation timestep. damping number Dimensionless Energy loss applied each simulation step. Values slightly below 1 are recommended. blurSigma number Dimensionless Gaussian blur strength used for postprocessing.

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: WaterSurface

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
local surface = Nami.create(
    100,
    100,
    0.5,
    20,
    1 / 50,
    0.995,
    1.1
)

Note: The timestep is expressed as 1 / physics FPS for easy interpretation. This is the recommended approach. Try to keep at or below 60.

Note: If the water looks spiky, the blur is too low. If the water lacks detail, the blur is too high.

docs.title: The CFL Condition (important)
docs.text: For stability, Nami will refuse to initialise if the given parameters do not satisfy the CFL condition: $$ \frac{wavespeed \times timestep}{vertspacing} \le \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} $$ For most use cases you probably wont have to worry about vertex spacing, just balancing time step to the wave speed you want. Faster waves require smaller step sizes.

docs.title: surface:initialise()
docs.text:
Creates the EditableMesh, MeshPart, and simulation buffers.

MUST be called before any other surface method.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: surface:initialise(parent)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description parent Instance Parent instance for the generated MeshPart.

docs.title: Example
docs.text: surface:initialise(workspace)

docs.title: surface:step()
docs.text: Advances the simulation. This should typically be called every frame from RunService.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text:
local didstep = surface:step(deltaTime)

Note: step() normally wont step everytime you call it. This is to make sure it runs at the defined FPS. It will return true if it did actually update the mesh.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description deltaTime number Frame delta time.

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: (boolean) -- Did run

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
RunService.Heartbeat:Connect(function(dt)
    surface:step(dt)
end)

docs.title: surface:destroy()
docs.text:
Destroys the generated mesh and marks the simulation as unusable.

After calling this method, any further operations on the surface will throw an error.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: surface:destroy()

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
surface:step()

surface:destroy()

surface:step() --Throws an error

docs.title: surface:worldPositionToMesh()
docs.text: Converts a world-space position into mesh grid coordinates. All mesh related functions (pointSplash, normalAt, heightAt, etc) take mesh grid coordinates. Use this function to convert world to mesh grid.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: local z, x = surface:worldPositionToMesh(worldZ, worldX)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description worldZ number World-space Z coordinate. worldX number World-space X coordinate.

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: (number, number)

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
local z, x = surface:worldPositionToMesh(
    hitPosition.Z,
    hitPosition.X
)

surface:pointSplash(z, x, 4)

__

docs.title: surface:normalAt()
docs.text:
Returns the surface normal at a grid coordinate.

Useful for buoyancy and object orientation.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text:
local z, x = surface:worldPositionToMesh(boat.Z, boat.X)
local normal = surface:normalAt(z, x)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type meshZ number meshX number

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: Vector3

docs.title: Example
docs.text: local normal = surface:normalAt(50, 50)

docs.title: surface:heightAt()
docs.text: Returns the rendered height of the water surface at a grid coordinate.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: local height = surface:heightAt(meshZ, meshX)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type meshZ number meshX number

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: number

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
local z, x = surface:worldPositionToMesh(boat.Z, boat.X)
local height = surface:heightAt(z, x)

docs.title: surface:pointSplash()
docs.text:
Injects a displacement into a single simulation cell.

Useful for impacts, raindrops, explosions, or object interaction.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: surface:pointSplash(z, x, height)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description z number Grid Z coordinate. x number Grid X coordinate. height number Initial wave displacement.

docs.title: Example
docs.text: surface:pointSplash(50, 50, 5)

docs.title: surface:smoothSplash()
docs.text:
Creates a smooth Gaussian-shaped disturbance.

Note: This function is currently not implemented.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: surface:smoothSplash()
